stem: Angle DEA is a right angle. Point B lies on side AD; point C lies on side AE. Segment BC is drawn so that angle BCA is a right angle, forming right triangle ABC inside right triangle ADE. In the figure, AB = 20 units, AC = 12 units, and CE = 18 units. What is the area, in square units, of triangle ADE?
rationale: ∠ACB and ∠AED are both right angles, and ∠BAC = ∠DAE (the same angle at A). Therefore triangles ABC and ADE are similar (AA). In △ABC, by the Pythagorean theorem, BC = √(20² − 12²) = √256 = 16. The corresponding side lengths give AE = AC + CE = 30 and the similarity ratio AE/AC = 30/12 = 5/2. So DE = BC × 5/2 = 16 × 5/2 = 40. The area of △ADE = (1/2)·AE·DE = (1/2)·30·40 = 600.
